The Conservation Unit code.  Conservation Unit (CU) is a group of wild Pacific salmon sufficiently isolated from other groups that, if extirpated, is very unlikely to recolonize naturally within an acceptable timeframe, such as a human lifetime or a specified number of salmon generations.
Conservation Unit from Ontology: https://w3id.org/gcdfo/salmon#ConservationUnit
A group of fish sufficiently isolated from other groups that, if extirpated, is very unlikely to recolonize naturally within an acceptable timeframe.

The assigned name of the Conservation Unit (CU). CAN BE ONE SINGULAR CU OR MULTIPLE, SEPARATED BY COMMAS

Notes:
SER = Sockeye (River Type)
SEL = Sockeye (Lake Type)
CM = Chum 
CO = Coho
PKE = Pink-Even
PKO = Pink-Odd
CK = Chinook
Definition: The unique ID to identify Conservation Units (CU). Units are alpha-numeric, the first part of the ID identifies the species followed by a number.
